symptoms.symptom_name: Unilateral painful, swollen, warm right calf (DVT features)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinical DVT is the source of the embolus and a major Wells criterion; asymmetry points to venous thrombosis over cellulitis or musculoskeletal cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden pleuritic chest pain worse on deep inspiration with acute dyspnoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pleuritic chest pain with sudden dyspnoea is a hallmark of PE with pulmonary infarction.
Pleuritic, breathing-dependent pain is atypical for ischaemic cardiac pain, though ACS cannot be excluded without ECG/troponin.
Haemoptysis reflects pulmonary infarction and is a Wells PE criterion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Patient-reported racing heart and severe breathlessness at rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Perceived tachycardia and resting dyspnoea are patient-obtainable proxies for the tachycardia and hypoxaemia of a significant PE.
Cancer is a strong independent VTE risk factor (hypercoagulable state) and a Wells criterion, raising pre-test probability substantially.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (HR 122), tachypnoea (RR 28), hypotension-trend (BP 105/65) and hypoxaemia (SpO2 91% room air)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective vitals showing tachycardia and hypoxia with a low-normal BP indicate a haemodynamically significant PE — clinician-measured, sealed; the AI infers compromise from the patient's reported breathlessness and racing heart, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear lung fields on auscultation despite profound hypoxia; loud P2 (acute right-heart strain)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypoxia with a clear chest plus a loud P2 is highly suggestive of PE with pulmonary hypertension/right-heart strain — auscultation is clinician-only and sealed.
Clear lung fields argue against a consolidating pneumonia as the cause of the hypoxia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive Homan's sign and tenderness along the deep popliteal venous system

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Calf tenderness supports DVT as the embolic source; Homan's sign is neither sensitive nor specific and is clinician-elicited (sealed).

symptoms.symptom_name: CT pulmonary angiogram filling defect (confirmatory imaging)

investigation_details.expected_result: Intraluminal filling defect in the pulmonary arterial tree confirming pulmonary embolism
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without imaging the embolus is neither confirmed nor localised; but imaging is a hospital investigation — the AI escalates rather than orders it.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A filling defect on CTPA is the confirmatory finding — obtainable only in hospital, never in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No preceding fever or viral prodrome

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The absence of a febrile prodrome makes an infective cause of the acute dyspnoea less likely, favouring an embolic event.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Unilateral painful calf swelling followed by sudden pleuritic chest pain, breathlessness and haemoptysis in an active-malignancy patient is a near-pathognomonic emergency pattern demanding immediate escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT pulmonary angiography (in hospital, via ED)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and localises the pulmonary embolism; arranged emergently through ED after 000 transport, not by the AI as an outpatient order.
Confirms the deep vein thrombosis source; a hospital/imaging investigation the AI cannot perform remotely.
To assess right-heart strain and exclude acute coronary syndrome — clinician/hospital investigations that the AI recognises the need for but cannot obtain.
